forecasts.reasoning: Anthropic recently closed a massive $3.5 billion private funding round in February 2025 at a valuation of $61.5 billion. It is highly irregular for a company to announce an IPO mere months after such a huge private capital raise. A comprehensive search across major financial media outlets (WSJ, Bloomberg, Reuters, CNBC, The Information) reveals no rumors, leaks, or reports of Anthropic hiring investment bankers or preparing a confidential S-1. Furthermore, multiple recent stock analysis pieces specifically highlight that Anthropic has no plans to go public and has not filed an S-1. With only six weeks remaining until the July 1, 2025 deadline, the probability of an unexpected S-1 filing or official IPO announcement on their news page is extremely low.

The Serbian government formed a new cabinet under Prime Minister Djuro Macut in April 2025, following the resignation of former PM Miloš Vučević in early 2025. Despite ongoing student and opposition protests demanding a transitional government and snap elections, the state leadership has explicitly rejected these demands. In March 2025, President Vučić firmly ruled out organizing early elections within six months, and in early May, key ruling party figures reinforced this stance, warning that early polls would be disastrous. Given that a new government was just installed and authorities deliberately passed on the constitutional opportunity to call elections when the previous PM stepped down, the likelihood of them reversing course to announce a specific general election date before the July 1, 2025 deadline is extremely low.

The resolution criteria ask if the Starlink Availability Map will have anywhere in Somalia listed as 'Available' before July 1, 2025. 

We gathered several strong, official pieces of evidence:
1. On April 13, 2025, the National Communications Authority of Somalia officially granted a license to Starlink.
2. The same day, Elon Musk announced on X (formerly Twitter) that Starlink is now available in Somalia.
3. According to a May 5, 2025 article discussing local reactions, Starlink has "officially expanded coverage to Somalia, according to publicly available service maps" and users can order the kits. 

Given that it was officially licensed, announced as 'available' by the CEO, and has reportedly already been launched on the service maps well before our forecast date of May 15, 2025, there is near total certainty that the map will list Somalia as available anytime before July 1, 2025 (and likely already does, which instantly satisfies the resolution criteria). I am keeping the probability slightly below 100% just to account for unforeseeable events or bizarre technicalities (e.g., retroactively revoking a license and taking it off the map, though that shouldn't affect the criteria if it was on the map at *any* point before July 1st). The only possible, highly improbable scenario for a NO resolution is that the map has oddly never updated despite all these formal announcements, and fails to do so for the next 1.5 months.

The evidence overwhelmingly points toward an imminent announcement of a nuclear agreement between the U.S. and Iran. In mid-March 2025, U.S. President Trump set a strict two-month deadline for a deal, which aligns perfectly with a conclusion in mid-to-late May. The 4th round of talks in Oman concluded on May 11 with the U.S. presenting a formal written proposal, and Iran's lead negotiator Araghchi returning to Tehran for consultations. Public statements from both sides indicate terms are virtually finalized: a top adviser to Ayatollah Khamenei told NBC News on May 14 that Iran is prepared to eliminate its highly enriched uranium stockpiles, and President Trump publicly stated on May 15 that the two nations have 'sort of agreed' to the terms. Given the impending deadline, the direct and active negotiations, and public commitment to major concessions from both parties, the likelihood that an agreement (interim or final) is announced before July 1, 2025, is exceptionally high.

Starlink secured its initial license approval in Chad in November 2024, at which time Elon Musk prematurely tweeted that the service was 'available.' The final, formal operating license was signed on March 13, 2025. Nearby Niger, which cleared similar regulatory hurdles around the exact same time, saw its consumer launch happen almost immediately. Although the Chadian government announced that the service would be available 'nationwide by the end of 2025', Starlink typically flips countries to 'Available' on its map as soon as it begins accepting commercial orders or a region becomes active, rather than waiting for full-scale nationwide penetration. Given that the major regulatory and licensing barriers are now resolved as of mid-March, Starlink has more than 3.5 months before the July 1, 2025 deadline to commence operations and update its map to 'Available.' The base rate for Starlink going live quickly after final operating license signatures is very high. Thus, a map update before July 1 is more likely than not, despite the 'end of 2025' timeline caveat referring to wider adoption.

The objective in the Keep_Track Project 2025 Tracker is to "Break up the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA)." As of mid-May 2025 (less than two months before the July 1 resolution date), the tracker still has this objective entirely blank, despite ongoing budget cuts, layoffs, and the termination of some advisory boards. The tracker is conservative about marking things complete—for example, major structural changes to the CFPB are marked "in progress." 

Crucially, in January 2025, Commerce Secretary nominee Howard Lutnick officially disavowed the Project 2025 plan to break up NOAA. Any actual attempt to break up the agency requires congressional approval (as noted in an April 2025 NYT report about White House budget plans to dismantle NOAA research), a process taking many months or years, especially with deep pushback from Democrats and some Republicans. Even if the administration pursues this heavily over the next six weeks, it would at most result in an "in progress" mark, not "complete," which is required for a YES resolution. The timeline is simply too tight for an objective of this magnitude to be achieved and marked complete by the Keep_Track operators.

After former PM Milos Vucevic resigned in January 2025, President Vucic had the choice to either call snap elections or form a new government. While snap elections were a real possibility early on, Vucic ultimately nominated Djuro Macut (a political outsider and medical professor) to be the new Prime Minister. Macut's government was successfully approved by parliament on April 16, 2025, holding a comfortable majority (153 of 250 seats). Despite ongoing protests from students demanding an immediate snap election, senior figures in the ruling party (including Vucevic) and President Vucic have explicitly rejected these demands as recently as May 6, stating that the path forward is through dialogue with the new PM, rather than returning to the polls. Given that a new government was formed only a month ago specifically to defuse tensions and avoid early elections, an unforeseen collapse leading to an election announcement within the incredibly narrow 45-day window before July 1, 2025, is highly improbable.

On April 13, 2025, Elon Musk announced that Starlink is now available in Somalia, and the Somalian government simultaneously confirmed they granted an operational license to SpaceX. Subsequent reports indicate that Starlink internet services have launched in the country, with one article from early May noting that Starlink had expanded coverage to Somalia 'according to publicly available service maps'. Starlink historically updates its availability map immediately upon announcing availability in a new country. There is no evidence suggesting it won't be on the map before July 1, 2025, and overwhelming evidence that it was already placed on the map as of mid-April.

In November 2024, Starlink secured initial regulatory approval to operate in Chad, and Elon Musk celebrated the upcoming launch. In mid-March 2025, Starlink officially signed its operator license in Chad, finalizing the regulatory process. The question asks if the Starlink Map will show Chad as 'Available' before July 1, 2025. Since the license was just finalized, Starlink has ~3.5 months (mid-March to end of June) to officially make the service commercially available and update its map status. Given that Chad is long overdue (originally slated for 2024) and the Chadian government is eager for the service to roll out to solve internet reliability issues, the technical and legal hurdles seem to be cleared. The main risk is bureaucratic or logistical delays similar to previous African rollouts, but 3.5 months from definitive license signing to online ordering is a generous timeline for Starlink. I estimate a 75% probability of YES.

Anthropic successfully raised large amounts of private funding as recently as Q1 2025, pushing its valuation to roughly $61.5 billion. Given the availability of vast private capital, there is very little financial necessity for Anthropic to pursue an IPO in the immediate term. Furthermore, major IPOs of this size require months of preparation, including hiring top-tier investment banks and usually a confidential S-1 process, both of which reliably leak to the press in advance (as seen with CoreWeave). With only 1.5 months remaining until the deadline of July 1, 2025, and zero credible rumors of banking appointments or S-1 preparations, an announcement is extremely improbable. I am capping my low probability at the system's requested floor of 0.05.

The objective to "break up NOAA" is primarily being pursued by the Trump administration through the FY2026 budget proposal, which includes an OMB passback to dismantle NOAA's research divisions and drastically cut its funding. Passing a federal budget or formally restructuring a statutory agency requires an act of Congress. Because the 2026 fiscal year does not begin until October 1, 2025, Congress will not finalize these appropriations before the resolution date of July 1, 2025. Additionally, the maintainers of the r/Keep_Track spreadsheet require definitive final actions (such as passed legislation or finalized, unchallenged rules) to mark an objective as "Complete" (✔), as evidenced by other major structural changes remaining "in progress" while courts or legislative processes play out. Given the 45-day window from the May 15 forecast date to the July 1 resolution date, it is highly improbable this objective will be marked as completely fulfilled.
